beginner.title: Have Got
beginner.content:
📌 Khi nào dùng HAVE GOT
Dùng have got để nói về:
• Sở hữu: I've got a new phone. She's got a dog.
• Mối quan hệ: He's got two sisters. We've got a great teacher.
• Đặc điểm / ngoại hình: She's got blue eyes. He's got short hair.
• Bệnh tật / triệu chứng: I've got a headache. She's got a cold.
⚠️ Have got = Have (cùng nghĩa sở hữu) nhưng have got phổ biến hơn trong tiếng Anh Anh (British English).
📐 Công thức đầy đủ
Chủ ngữKhẳng địnhRút gọnPhủ địnhCâu hỏi
I / You / We / Theyhave gotI've gothave not got / haven't gotHave you got…?
He / She / Ithas gotHe's gothas not got / hasn't gotHas she got…?
✅ Trả lời ngắn (Short answers)
Have you got a pen? – Yes, I have. / No, I haven't.
Has she got a car? – Yes, she has. / No, she hasn't.
⚠️ Không nói: ~~Yes, I've got.~~ → Phải nói: Yes, I have.
🔄 Have got vs Have (American English)
• British: I've got a car. → American: I have a car.
• British: Have you got time? → American: Do you have time?
• Cả hai đều đúng trong tiếng Anh hiện đại. Trong bài thi quốc tế, chấp nhận cả hai.
⚠️ Lỗi thường gặp
❌ She have got a dog. → ✅ She has got a dog.
❌ Has she got? Yes, she's got. → ✅ Yes, she has.
❌ I haven't got no money. → ✅ I haven't got any money.
❌ Do you have got a pen? → ✅ Have you got a pen?
❌ He haves got blue eyes. → ✅ He has got blue eyes.
I've got two brothers and one sister.Tôi có hai anh và một em gái.
Has she got a car? – No, she hasn't.Cô ấy có xe không? – Không.
He's got short brown hair and green eyes.Anh ấy có tóc nâu ngắn và mắt xanh.

exercises.q: She ___ got a beautiful garden.
___ you got a minute? I need to ask you something.
They ___ got any milk left. We need to buy some.
He ___ got a new job at a big company in the city.
Have you got a pen? – Yes, I ___.
exercises.exp: Short answer: Yes, I have. (không dùng I've got)

exercises.q: My sister ___ got long dark hair and brown eyes.
We ___ got enough chairs for all the guests tonight.
___ your parents got a big house?
exercises.exp: Your parents (= they) → Have they got…?

exercises.q: I ___ got a terrible headache. I need some medicine.
Has the hotel got a swimming pool? – Yes, it ___.
She ___ got three cats and a dog at home.
___ he got a driving licence yet?
We've got ___ new neighbours. They moved in last week.
I ___ got any cash on me. Can I pay by card?
The baby ___ got beautiful blue eyes like her mother.
___ the school got a sports hall?
exercises.exp: The school (= it) → Has it got…?

exercises.q: He have got a new bicycle. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.ans: Wrong – correct: He has got a new bicycle
exercises.exp: He/She/It → has got (không phải have got)

exercises.q: Have you got any brothers or sisters? → Correct or Wrong?
Yes, I've got. → Correct or Wrong?
exercises.exp: Short answer không dùng rút gọn 've got

exercises.q: She hasn't got any money in her purse. → Correct or Wrong?
I ___ got a great idea for the project!
___ she got a spare key to the flat?
He ___ got a meeting this afternoon, so he can't join us.
We ___ got enough time to finish everything before the deadline.
Have you got a dictionary? – No, I ___.
My parents ___ got a lovely cottage by the sea.
She ___ got beautiful long hair that reaches her waist.
The flat ___ got three bedrooms and a large balcony.
___ your brother got a job yet?
exercises.exp: Your brother (= he) → Has he got…?

exercises.q: I ___ got any idea what she's talking about.
exercises.exp: haven't got any = I don't know at all

exercises.q: Has the town got a good market? – Yes, it ___.
They ___ got two children — a boy and a girl.
He ___ got short black hair and dark brown eyes.
We ___ got a new coffee machine in the office. Everyone loves it.
___ you got a moment? I need your advice about something.
She ___ got a cold, so she isn't coming to school today.
They ___ got any pets. Their flat doesn't allow animals.
My grandmother ___ got a huge collection of old photographs.
___ the hotel got free Wi-Fi for guests?
exercises.exp: The hotel (= it) → Has it got…?

exercises.q: I've got ___ good news and some bad news. Which do you want first?
exercises.exp: Khẳng định với danh từ không đếm được → some

reorder.words: She has got two cats and a small dog
Have you got a moment I need your help
He has got short dark hair and brown eyes
We haven't got enough chairs for all the guests
Has your sister got a driving licence yet
I have got a terrible headache today
They have got a lovely house near the park
Has the school got a swimming pool
She hasn't got any money left in her purse
Have you got any brothers or sisters
My parents have got a cottage by the sea
He has got a new job at a big company
The flat has got three bedrooms and a large balcony
I haven't got a clue what she is talking about
Has the restaurant got a vegetarian menu
We have got some great news to share with you
Has he got a moment to speak with me
She has got beautiful green eyes like her mother
They haven't got any pets because their flat does not allow animals
Have you got the time I have left my watch at home

passage.text: Hi! My name is Mia. I {{1}} a big family. My mum {{2}} three sisters, so I {{3}} three aunts. My dad {{4}} two brothers. I {{5}} one younger brother called Tom. Tom {{6}} dark curly hair and he {{7}} freckles on his nose. Our house {{8}} a big garden where we grow vegetables. We {{9}} two cats and a rabbit. The rabbit {{10}} very long ears and a white tail. {{11}} you got any pets at home?

I {{1}} a new smartphone but it {{2}} a case, so I need to buy one. The phone {{3}} an excellent camera — it {{4}} three lenses on the back. It {{5}} face recognition too, which is very convenient. However, it {{6}} a headphone jack, which is a problem for me because I {{7}} wireless earphones. My friend Sam {{8}} the same phone and she loves it. She says the battery {{9}} great life and {{10}} any serious problems. I am happy with my purchase but I wish it {{11}} a longer warranty.

The town of Millbrook is small but charming. It {{1}} a beautiful old church in the centre. The church {{2}} a tall tower that you can see from miles away. The town {{3}} two supermarkets and a weekly market on Saturdays. It {{4}} a cinema or a theatre, unfortunately. However, it {{5}} a wonderful community centre. The centre {{6}} a gym, a café and a library. The library {{7}} thousands of books and it {{8}} free Wi-Fi for visitors. The park {{9}} a lake where people go fishing. The local school {{10}} excellent sports facilities. The town {{11}} much nightlife, but most residents say they love the peace and quiet.

My best friend Leo is very interesting. He {{1}} a passion for cooking and {{2}} over two hundred cookbooks at home. He {{3}} a large kitchen with professional equipment. He {{4}} a food blog too, which is very popular. He {{5}} thousands of followers online. He {{6}} a part-time job at a restaurant on weekends. The restaurant {{7}} a Michelin star, so the standards are very high. Leo says he {{8}} a dream to open his own café one day. He {{9}} already found the perfect location. I {{10}} a very lucky friend because he always cooks for me. We both {{11}} the same taste in food, which makes things easy!

The new sports centre in our neighbourhood {{1}} something for everyone. It {{2}} an Olympic-size swimming pool and four tennis courts. The gym {{3}} the latest equipment and very helpful staff. However, it {{4}} a car park, which is a problem for many visitors. The café inside {{5}} healthy food and smoothies. It {{6}} a children's area where kids can play safely. The centre {{7}} classes for all ages — yoga, dance, and martial arts. I {{8}} a monthly membership and I love it. My sister {{9}} hers yet because she {{10}} enough money at the moment. She {{11}} a part-time job, so hopefully she can join soon.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về bản thân: bạn có gì (đồ vật, thú cưng, gia đình)?
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: have got / has got – khẳng định
writing_prompts.example: I've got a small but cosy room. I've got a laptop and lots of books. My family has got a dog called Max. We've got a garden where Max loves to play.

writing_prompts.vi: Hỏi và trả lời về những thứ bạn không có (phủ định) hoặc đặt câu hỏi về người khác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: haven't got / hasn't got / Have you got…?
writing_prompts.example: I haven't got a car, so I always take the bus. My friend Tom hasn't got a mobile phone — he prefers books! Have you got a moment to talk? Has she got a spare key?

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả ngoại hình của một người bạn biết dùng have got.
writing_prompts.example: My best friend Ana has got long dark hair and big brown eyes. She has got a warm smile that makes everyone feel welcome. She hasn't got any brothers or sisters, but she has got lots of friends.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về ngôi nhà hoặc thành phố của bạn dùng have got.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: has got for places and features
writing_prompts.example: My city has got a beautiful old cathedral and a large river. It has got lots of cafés and restaurants. Unfortunately, it hasn't got a big airport. Our house has got three bedrooms and a small garden.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn hội thoại ngắn hỏi và trả lời về sở hữu dùng Have got.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: Questions and short answers with have got
writing_prompts.example:
A: Have you got a pen I can borrow?
B: Yes, I have. Here you go.
A: Has your sister got a car?
B: No, she hasn't. She goes everywhere by bike.
A: Have you got any plans this weekend?
B: No, I haven't. Why?
